converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Edward Ostrander papers
Ostrander, Edward.
23-16-3259
4.4 cubic feet. 4.4 cubic feet.
1967-1977. Bibliographies, site study proposals and reports, correspondence, clippings, articles, transcripts, memos, training materials, notes, photographic contact sheets, negatives. Documents Ostrander's study of architecture and planning in senior housing and its effect on the aged. Collection material in English 4600835
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Justine Rosa
Date completed:
April 2003
EAD encoding:
J. Rosa, J.M. Nicholas, July 2003
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, August 2015
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor in the Dept. of Design and Environmental Analysis, Cornell University.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, publications, reports and subject files relating to Ostrander's work in gerontology and nursing homes, particularly the New York State Veterans' Home in Oxford.
SERIES LIST
A. Through 1970
Box 1, Folders 1-14
B. 1971
Box 1, Folders 15-30
C. 1972-1977
Box 1, Folders 31-42
Cite As:
Edward Ostrander papers, #23-16-3259. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Related Materials
See also collections #23-2-2817, #47-10-3394 and #23-16-1472 for additional biographical information.
Barrier-free design for older people. Nursing homes -- New York (State) -- Planning. New York State College of Human Ecology. Design and Environmental Analysis New York State Veterans\' Home (Oxford, N.Y.) Cornell University -- : Faculty.
